pending.body: The round-trip struct-drift tests (`start_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render` and `completion_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render`) populate all optional fields with `Some` values, which correctly catches missing fields via the re-render equality assertion. However, neither `StartJournalDetails` nor `CompletionJournalDetails` uses `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]`. This means that in production (outside the test), if a new field is added to the model's render function but not to the fs adapter's parsing struct, parsing will silently succeed and `merge()` will silently discard the unrecognized field — the exact failure mode Issue 1 describes. The round-trip test catches this in CI, but adding `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` to both `StartJournalDetails` and `CompletionJournalDetails` would also catch it at runtime, providing defense-in-depth. If forward-compatibility with newer journal formats is a concern, this could be gated behind a test-only assertion instead.

run_snapshot.status_summary: running: final review restart round 4 -> Plan and Implement
